adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (bet365 NED -150 -> de-vig ~58/24/18), with a slight nudge toward Sweden. Netherlands only drew Japan 2-2 in their opener and goalkeeper Verbruggen is questionable, while Sweden arrive on a high after thumping Tunisia 5-1 (Isak goal + assist). The Dutch are still rightful favourites with Gakpo leading the line, but this is no formality -- both teams should score. A 2-1 Netherlands win is the central case; the model's 81% is too high.

factors.note: Model 81% vs market 58%; anchored to market
5-1 demolition of Tunisia in opener; Isak in form
GK Verbruggen questionable; only drew Japan 2-2 in opener

adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (GER -180 / draw +350 / CIV +450 -> de-vig ~61/21/17). Germany opened with a 7-1 demolition of Curacao off 26 shots and are clear favourites, but Ivory Coast are level on points after a well-drilled 1-0 defensive win over Ecuador, so this is tougher than the scoreline of Germany's opener suggests. A controlled 2-0 Germany win is the central case; the model's 82% overstates it against an organised CIV side.

adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (ECU 1/10 / draw +1000 / CUW +2600 -> de-vig ~88/9/4). Both lost their openers (Ecuador 0-1 to Cote d'Ivoire -- their first defeat since facing Brazil in 2024 -- and Curacao 1-7 to Germany), so this is must-win for both, and an urgent Ecuador faces a Curacao defence that has already shipped seven. A comfortable Ecuador win, three-plus goals, is the central case; the model's 96% is a touch high but the market agrees Ecuador are overwhelming.

adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (JPN -190 / draw +310 / TUN +600 -> de-vig ~63/23/14). Japan arrive with momentum after a 2-2 draw with the Netherlands (late Kamada equaliser), while Tunisia were thumped 5-1 by Sweden and sacked coach Lamouchi, bringing in Herve Renard -- a side in disarray. Japan have won all three prior meetings, scoring six and conceding none. The baseline model's 95% Japan was far too extreme; market ~63% is the right level. A 0-2 Japan win is the central case.

factors.note: Model 95% vs market 63%; anchored to market (away favourite)

Sacked Lamouchi after 5-1 loss; Renard in mid-group -> disruption
